mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and pushing S&P 500 returns positive over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0%, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and a contraction in real consumer credit signals a supply-constrained demand boom that inflates corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates compress equity valuations unless offset by accelerating real GDP growth, which sustains earnings momentum and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single quarter
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >60% of triggered instances despite rising GDP, or the conditional IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects rising household confidence and construction activity, which directly leads positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as related sector earnings are repriced.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST growth rates over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal's hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month, it signals a supply-side contraction that typically precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising employment and stagnant short-term rates indicates accommodative financial conditions supporting labor demand, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as wage-driven consumption fuels earnings.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over any 18-month rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline distribution.

mechanism_narrative: In an inflationary demand-pull environment, rising consumer prices outpace nominal wage growth, compressing real disposable income and reducing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the conditional IC exceeds 0.03 in the opposite direction over a 2-year rolling window.
